overview: The GENESIS Program (Gen-Next Support for Innovative Startups) is a Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) initiative aimed at boosting the startup ecosystem in Tier-II and Tier-III cities across India. With a budgetary outlay of Rs. 490 Crore over 5 years, the scheme supports approximately 1600 tech startups by providing funding and support across pilot, early-stage, deep-tech, and investment stages. It focuses on consolidating MeitY's startup-related schemes and assets for greater efficiency and enhanced support.
objectives: Consolidation of startup related schemes and assets of MeitY for greater efficiency, asset utilization and enhanced support for startups.
Provide Tier-II/ III cities focused funding to critically support Pilot/ Investment, Early-stage and deep-tech startups.
Strengthening Incubators, building stronger startup ecosystem via capacity building initiatives in Tier II/ III cities.
Support approx. 1600 tech start-ups over the course of next 5 years
Discover, grow and nurture technology startups in Tier-II and Tier-III cities
eligibility: The scheme is designed for technology startups in Tier-II and Tier-III cities; specific eligibility criteria for individual components (EiR, Pilot Funding, Investment Support, Deep-Tech Funding) are not detailed in the provided evidence.
benefits: Support includes Entrepreneur in Residence (EiR) for idea validation, Pilot Funding for product piloting, Investment Support for market fundraising, and Deep-Tech Funding for high-impact solutions; non-financial benefits include mentorship, capacity building, and ecosystem strengthening in Tier-II/III cities.
financial_support: The scheme provides financial support through four components: Entrepreneur in Residence (EiR) Support, Pilot Funding Support, Investment Support, and Deep-Tech Funding Support, targeting startups at various stages from idea validation to scaling deep-tech solutions.
application_process: The application process is not explicitly detailed in the provided evidence; interested startups are advised to refer to the Implementing Agencies list and official links such as the Administrative Approval and Scheme Brochure (attached PDFs) for application procedures.
implementing_agency: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), Government of India
caveats: The scheme is focused on Tier-II and Tier-III cities; startups outside these regions may not be eligible.
Support is limited to approximately 1600 tech startups over 5 years.
The scheme duration is 5 years from approval.
